label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem step by step. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. We need to find the date one year ago from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, so I need to check which one is correct. First, let me clarify the birth date. The last day of February is usually the 28th, except in leap years when it's the 29th. But 2001 is not a leap year because leap years are divisible by 4. 2000 was a leap year, then 2004, so 2001 isn't. Therefore, the last day of February 2001 is February 28, 2001. So Jane's birthday is 02/28/2001. Now, today is her 16th birthday. That means today's date is 02/28/2017, because 2001 + 16 = 2017. Wait, wait. Wait, if she was born on 02/28/2001, then her first birthday would be 02/28/2002. So adding 16 years would make her 16th birthday on 02/28/2017. So today is 02/28/2017. The question is asking for the date one year ago from today. So subtract one year from 02/28/2017. That would be 02/28/2016. Let me check if 2016 is a leap year. 2016 is divisible by 4 (2016 ÷ 4 = 504), so yes, it is a leap year. But since we are just subtracting a year, regardless of leap years, the date one year before 02/28/2017 is 02/28/2016. However, if the date was March 1, 2017, and you subtract a year, it would be March 1, 2016. But here it's February 28, so even if 2016 is a leap year, the date remains the same. Because the 28th exists in both leap and non-leap years. So one year ago from 02/28/2017 would be 02/28/2016. Looking at the options, option C is 02/28/2016. Let me check if there's any trick here. But wait, sometimes when you subtract a year from someone's birthday, if they were born on February 29, then in non-leap years, their birthday is considered February 28. But Jane was born on the last day of February 2001, which is February 28. So in 2016, which is a leap year, the last day of February is February 29. But Jane's birthday is still February 28, regardless of leap years. Therefore, one year ago from today (which is her 16th birthday on 02/28/2017) is 02/28/2016. So option C is correct. Let me check the other options to make sure. Option A is 02/28/2015. That's two years before 2017, so no. Option B is March 31, 2016 – that seems unrelated. Option
